title: [InstCombine] Pull unary shuffles through fneg/fabs
summary:
The developers generalized the pattern by recognizing that `fneg(shuffle(x, mask))` can be transformed into `shuffle(fneg(x), mask)` for any shuffle, not just specific matrix multiply cases. They implemented this in `InstCombineAddSub.cpp` by adding a direct match for shuffles within `visitFNeg`. This replaces the previous approach in `InstCombineVectorOps.cpp`, which only handled unary shuffles and required one-use constraints, limiting broader applicability.

The key insight was to decouple the optimization from the original matrix multiply context and apply it as a generic canonicalization: moving `fneg` across a shuffle is always valid and simplifies subsequent folds. This also allowed removal of special-case logic for `fabs` and other unary ops, streamlining the codebase. The generalization now handles arbitrary shuffle masks and vector widths, enabling the transformation across diverse test cases including splat constants and mixed float/int operations.
